text: The European Parliament approved a landmark digital markets regulation targeting anticompetitive practices by large technology platforms operating across member states.
European Union member states negotiated binding emissions targets and a carbon border adjustment mechanism as part of the broader green transition legislative package.
The United Kingdom Parliament debated a trade bill governing post-Brexit customs arrangements, tariffs, and regulatory alignment with continental European markets.
A joint defense resolution called for increased military cooperation, coordinated sanctions, and a unified response to escalating geopolitical tensions on the eastern frontier.
Ministers proposed reforms to the asylum and migration pact, establishing shared responsibility mechanisms and border processing centers among participating nations.
British lawmakers passed an energy security bill accelerating investment in offshore wind, nuclear power, and domestic renewable generation to reduce reliance on imported gas.
